title: Wanaka Hidden Gems
meta_desc: Discover Wanaka's best-kept secrets for families, from scenic hikes to free activities, and create unforgettable memories with your kids in this stunning NZ town.
intro: Wanaka, the quieter and more beautiful neighbour of Queenstown, is a haven for families seeking adventure and relaxation. While many flock to its famous attractions, there are plenty of hidden gems waiting to be uncovered. In this article, we'll reveal the lesser-known spots and insider tips that will make your family holiday in Wanaka truly unforgettable.

sections.heading: Diamond Lake and Rocky Mountain Track
sections.body:
Diamond Lake and the Rocky Mountain track offer a 3-hour loop walk that's perfect for families with kids aged 7 and above. The track starts 20 minutes from Wanaka and rewards hikers with breathtaking views of Lake Wanaka and the Matukituki Valley.

To get there, drive 20 minutes from Wanaka towards Glendhu Bay and turn left onto the unsealed road. Parking is available near the track entrance. The walk is moderately difficult and costs nothing, making it an excellent free activity for families.

sections.heading: Wanaka's Lone Willow Tree at Sunset
sections.body:
Wanaka's famous lone willow tree is a must-visit, especially during sunset or early morning when the light is golden and the crowds are smaller. Avoid visiting at midday when it's packed with photographers.

To get the best shot, arrive about an hour before sunset and be prepared for a short walk along the lake shore. This activity is free and suitable for all ages.
list: Best time to visit: Sunset or early morning

sections.heading: Glendhu Bay Swim Beach
sections.body:
Glendhu Bay is a stunning swim beach located just 10 minutes from Wanaka town. The beach is perfect for families with kids of all ages, offering calm and clear waters, as well as a picturesque backdrop of mountains and trees.

To get there, drive towards Glendhu Bay and follow the signs to the beach. Parking is available near the beach entrance, and there are no fees to visit.

sections.heading: Insider Tips for Families
sections.body:
For families visiting Wanaka, it's essential to plan ahead and book accommodations and activities in advance, especially during peak season.

Consider renting a house or apartment instead of a hotel room to save money and have more space for your family. Additionally, take advantage of the many free activities in Wanaka, such as the Wanaka Lakeshore walk and the Mt Iron track, to keep costs down.
list: Book accommodations and activities in advance
Rent a house or apartment instead of a hotel room
